# Daily Report — 2026-04-13

## Task Completed
Implemented the `create_ui_policy_action` tool (`sys_ui_policy_action` table).
A UI policy action specifies what happens to a single form field when its parent
UI policy's condition evaluates to true. The tool exposes three independent
field-behaviour controls — `mandatory`, `visible`, and `disabled` (read-only) —
each accepting `"true"`, `"false"`, or `"leave_alone"` (default). The action is
linked to its parent policy via `ui_policy_id`.

Changes:
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/ui_policy_tools.py` — added `FieldBehaviour` type
  alias, `CreateUIPolicyActionParams`, `UIPolicyActionResponse`, and
  `create_ui_policy_action` function
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — exported `create_ui_policy_action`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — imported and registered the new tool
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — enabled `create_ui_policy_action` in
  `catalog_builder`, `platform_developer`, and `full` packages (removed TODO stubs)
- `tests/test_ui_policy_tools.py` — added 10 new test cases covering defaults,
  all field-behaviour combinations, endpoint targeting, HTTP/connection errors,
  Pydantic validation, and response model construction

## Test Results
- Total tests: 419 passed, 4 deselected
- Coverage: 88% overall; ui_policy_tools.py 100%
- All passing: yes

Pre-existing advisory-level findings in infrastructure packages (pip, setuptools,
